title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's home at 1210 Washington St., built in 1950, was sold for $500,000.
A Boston high-rise condo at 100 Belvidere St. sold for $3,700,000, the highest listed in the report.
A three-family Andover property at 20-22 Florence St. sold for $1,410,000.

title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are considering Colts wideout Alec Pierce as a potential free-agent target this offseason.
Indianapolis tagged QB Daniel Jones, leaving Alec Pierce available for free agency instead of receiving the franchise tag.
Pierce, leading the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025, could command $25-30 million per year.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, 34, of North Troy, Vermont, was cited for throwing away mail she was supposed to deliver.
Vermont State Police found mail in a dumpster on Elm Street, identifying Morisseau as the postal employee responsible.
Morisseau faces nine petty larceny counts and five unlawful mischief counts; she's suspended and due in court March 17.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An elderly man and a dog died in a house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough early Tuesday.
Firefighters from Foxborough and nearby towns extinguished the fire, which caused major damage to the single-family home.
The cause of the fire, reported just before 3 a.m., is currently under investigation by local authorities.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Boston Licensing Board is reviewing Estella's restaurant liquor license after a party with Patriots players involved alleged violations.
Owner Helder George Brandao blamed Patriots players for bringing strippers and hookahs to Estella's after-hours celebration.
Police found 30 people, hookahs, strippers, and alcohol violations at the private party, leading to potential license suspension.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest nationwide scores on Advanced Placement exams, setting a new all-time record.
In Massachusetts, 35.8% of public high school graduates scored 3 or higher on an AP exam in 2025.
The state supports AP exam participation by subsidizing fees and promoting Early College programs for high school students.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy's seven new city councilors are receiving a $15,372 raise, despite campaigning to repeal it.
Some councilors plan to donate their raises due to restrictions from state laws and the Quincy city charter.
The raise resulted from a prior council's decision, faced with public disappointment and procedural complaints.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox consider trading outfielder Jarren Duran for Astros' Isaac Paredes, despite recent roster adjustments.
Isaac Paredes hit 20 home runs in 102 games with the Astros last season, noted as a right-handed power option.
Trade talks for Paredes remain unlikely as Red Sox have not contacted the Astros since acquiring Caleb Durbin.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's mock draft has Patriots selecting Texas A&M's Cashius Howell at 31st overall as a pass-rushing force.
Cashius Howell led the SEC with 41 pressures and recorded 11.5 sacks, ranking seventh among FBS defensive linemen.
Despite questions about his frame, Howell's skills could make him a designated rusher for New England's defense.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, was convicted of murdering Salem State University student Claire Gravel in 1986 in Essex County Court.
Carey was indicted in 2022 after new DNA evidence linked him to Gravel's murder weapon, a black tank top.
Carey, already in prison for a separate attempted murder, now faces a life sentence without parole for Gravel's murder.
